subject: the seven-stage engineering protocol itself
not_subject: whether branch protection works — GitHub already documents that
hypothesis_Hp: the seven-stage protocol remains sufficient when a mandatory authority boundary interrupts execution, WITHOUT requiring modification to the protocol itself
success_criterion: an EXISTING stage terminates in a state such as BLOCKED_AWAITING_AUTHORITY, expressing work-completed + authority-required + observation-paused, with NO eighth stage added
falsification: if a new lifecycle stage is genuinely required, the protocol was incomplete. That outcome is equally informative and must be recorded as such.
why_stronger_than_concurrency: concurrency tests a different technical SUBJECT; a founder-gated control tests a different governance TOPOLOGY
independence_note: pre-registered before observation, unlike T3b whose predictions were authored after the unit tests encoded the same expectations
result: PENDING — decisive observation (clean traversal of stages 4-7 after founder action) has not occurred

stage_log.name: Observation
stage_log.result: branch protection OBSERVED_ABSENT (GitHub 404); commit signing OBSERVED_ABSENT (0/20 verifiable)

stage_log.name: Bounded repair
stage_log.reason: repair is gh api PUT .../protection + signing key creation — repo admin and credential creation, both founder-gated
stage_log.agent_work_completed: transition predicate built and tested (scripts/authority_gate.py, 17 tests)

secondary_finding.status: ESTABLISHED — holds regardless of how Hp resolves
secondary_finding.observed: the protocol had no explicit representation of a resume trigger after an authority-gated pause
secondary_finding.classification: MISSING TRANSITION, not a missing stage
secondary_finding.distinction: stages describe what work EXISTS; transitions describe when work becomes ELIGIBLE
secondary_finding.remedy: scripts/authority_gate.py — a transition predicate between stage 3 and stage 4
